description: Paired 2000-Agent API-backed rest-day experiment with the same 360-vehicle fleet and behavior parameters as the workday experiment.

outdoor_exposure.bus: origin access walk plus wait plus destination access walk
outdoor_exposure.metro: station access and egress walking only; platform wait is indoors
outdoor_exposure.w2_rain_method: outdoor minutes overlapping active heavy-rain windows

scale_definition.interpretation: The rest-day run holds the 360-vehicle fleet constant to isolate day-type demand and timing effects. It is not a calibrated Shanghai weekend fleet estimate.

scope_notes: The representative rest day is 2026-07-11, as configured in the formal nine-zone transport model.
W1 uses the all-day UTCI curve and the 11:00-18:00 behavioral heat window; W2 uses the configured rest-day 10:00-14:00 rain window.
The same seed, Agents, seven-day activity plans, transport rules and 360-vehicle spatial pool are shared across paired policy scenarios.
Holding the fleet equal to the workday experiment is an experimental control. A lower weekend fleet should be studied separately as a supply sensitivity, not mixed into the main day-type comparison.
Bus and metro boarding remain guaranteed; only ride-hailing has a finite conserved vehicle pool.
Every completed platform run is postprocessed offline for heat and rain exposure without additional LLM calls.
